sections.title: Operational Failure M&M: Reframed Opportunity and Peer Differential

depth_delta.retained_on_slide: Module 1 closes by moving from mindset to leadership identity.
depth_delta.deferred_depth: The workbook should carry the detailed applied practice: chief complaint framing, 5 Whys root-cause analysis, reframed opportunity, peer blind-spot review, and organizational-resistance differential diagnosis.

exercises.prompt_intent: Learner selects a recurring operational, clinical, or administrative failure, writes a one-sentence chief complaint, performs a brief 5 Whys analysis, and reframes the complaint as an actionable opportunity.

Learner reviews two peers' analyses, identifies one blind spot, and offers a constructive differential diagnosis for organizational resistance using Sandra Lam's Intrapreneurship Formula or psychological-safety concepts.

sections.narrative_intent: Convert the Canvas discussion prompt into workbook guidance that helps learners diagnose operational failure without blaming individuals or jumping to solutions.

sections.title: Comprehensive Module Knowledge Check Review

depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The bridge slide summarizes Module 1 and points to Module 2.
depth_delta.deferred_depth: The workbook should carry answer rationales and remediation notes for the comprehensive Module 1 knowledge check.

exercises.prompt_intent: Reinforce the distinction between invention and innovation as adopted systemic value.
Reinforce how clinical diagnosis maps to design thinking: history-taking to Empathize, synthesis to Define, differential diagnosis to Ideate, and treatment planning to Prototype.

sections.narrative_intent: Build a compact workbook review section from the knowledge-check answer explanations, emphasizing misconceptions that would weaken innovation framing.

sections.title: Innovation Leader Playbook: Opportunity Radar

depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The deck can introduce the bridge from mindset to identity.
depth_delta.deferred_depth: The workbook should carry the detailed playbook work: personal SWOT, goals and fears, initial idea portfolio, and five opportunity-radar problem statements.

exercises.prompt_intent: Learner evaluates mindset, motivations, work environment, fixed-mindset traits, and psychological safety on their primary team.
Learner creates an Opportunity Radar by identifying five specific problems, frictions, or opportunities for improvement in their physician work environment.

sections.narrative_intent: Turn the playbook prompt and rubric into a learner-ready workbook section that makes the assessment actionable and scannable.

research_goals.pedagogical_intent: The workbook should remain source-grounded to the Part 4 assessment, playbook, rubric, and bridge files, and should explicitly avoid presenting missing PDF, deck, image, DOI, or rendered-motion material as if it were present.
